response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack and systems software engineer with a broad career spanning web, mobile, embedded, and low-latency server applications, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position requires deep hardware expertise — SoC and IP modeling, GPU architecture, AI accelerator hardware-software co-design, and deep neural network workload optimization — none of which appear anywhere in his resume. His most recent AI-adjacent work at Naver is limited to software-layer MCP tooling for agentic workflows, and his only hardware experience involves robotic arm control software written in DLang and Java. He meets the minimum years-of-experience bar via his Bachelor's degree path, but lacks the entire technical domain the role demands.
strengths: 16+ years of continuous technical growth from junior developer to staff-level IC across diverse domains, demonstrating strong engineering fundamentals
Performance-conscious background at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic building low-latency async I/O systems handling millions of HTTP requests per second
Exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and developer tooling at Naver, showing awareness of the current AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero SoC or IP modeling experience — the primary required technical skill for the role
No GPU architecture, SIMD/MIMD, or AI accelerator experience of any kind; the entire hardware optimization domain is absent from the resume
No background in deep learning training or inference optimization; AI exposure is purely at the application and tooling layer
